What We've Done With Your Brand
Proof it works. From your own portfolio.

Mountain Dew needed to drive trial of their newest flavor innovation, Dragon Fruit, among college students — reaching them in the housing complexes and campus venues where they live and study. Here's how Recess combined physical sampling, social amplification, and a Walmart-linked purchase strategy to deliver real, measurable results.

Samples Distributed
161,519
Mountain Dew — Dragon Fruit Launch

14,112 samples across 27 college venues, launching Mountain Dew Dragon Fruit with a $5.10 iROAS.

Mountain Dew launched their newest innovation, Mountain Dew Dragon Fruit, and added sampling to their Walmart innovation strategy — reaching college residents across 27 different venues, driving trial of the new flavor and achieving measurable return on ad spend.

Why Recess
Six capabilities that make Recess different.

Recess isn't another sampling company. It's a full-funnel, retailer-accountable trial platform — built on trusted venues, authentic distribution, omnichannel amplification, and closed-loop measurement.

01
Scaled Network of Lifestyle Venues
10,000+ gyms, universities, coworking spaces, apartments, and events — geo-targeted around priority retail locations.
02
Authentic Community Distribution
Products delivered by trusted venue staff — not brand ambassadors or street teams — at the exact moment consumers are forming new habits.
03
Omnichannel Amplification
Every physical sample is amplified by venue social posts, targeted email, in-app messaging, and on-site signage — a media plan built into every activation.
04
Retailer-Aligned Targeting
Every activation is geo-targeted around high-density retailer locations — campuses near Walmart, gyms near Target, apartments near Kroger — so trial converts to shelf.
05
Closed-Loop Retail Measurement
Purchase intent, new-to-brand conversion, iROAS, and incremental sales lift — measured through consumer insights surveys and third-party retail data.
06
Moments That Shape Habits
College move-in. Post-workout. Morning at the coworking lounge. Activations timed to the exact occasions when consumers build new brand loyalties.

The Playbook
How a Recess campaign works

Four integrated steps — from venue matching to closed-loop measurement — that turn a single activation into full-funnel, measurable growth.

1
The Network
We match your brand to 10,000+ trusted venues — gyms, universities, coworking spaces, apartments — where your audience already gathers.
2
Authentic Engagement
Trusted community staff hand-deliver your product at the exact moment consumers are forming habits.
3
Omnichannel Amplification
Every sample is amplified by venue social posts, targeted emails, in-app messaging, and on-site signage.
4
Measured Results
Consumer insights, purchase intent, new-to-brand conversion, and social amplification — measured and reported every campaign.
Campaign Results
Real campaigns. Real PepsiCo brands. Real results.

From college campuses to fitness studios to coworking spaces — Recess has activated sampling campaigns across the PepsiCo portfolio, reaching the right consumers at the right moments.

2024
161,519
samples across 140+ WeWork locations nationwide
Rockstar Energy
Rockstar Energy & Focus at WeWork Nationwide

Rockstar partnered with Recess to sample Rockstar Energy and Rockstar Focus across 140+ WeWork locations nationwide — reaching young professionals in their daily routine, driving product trial and brand consideration at scale.

“This was the perfect pick-me-up between meetings — I had no idea they had a Focus line and now I’m hooked. — Rockstar sampler
2023
9,915
samples to ~11,000 consumers in LA fitness & coworking
Poppi
Fitness & Coworking Activation in Los Angeles

Poppi partnered with fitness studios and coworking spaces across Los Angeles to drive brand awareness and product trial, reaching nearly 11,000 health-conscious consumers in contextually relevant environments.

“This was the best and most interactive activation we have ever had — members were excited to try the flavors AND take photos to share! — WeWork One Culver
2024
91,872
bottles distributed across 7 universities
Pure Leaf
Blackberry Tea at College Rec Centers & Food Halls

Pure Leaf partnered with Recess to distribute over 91,000 bottles of Blackberry Tea across seven universities, reaching 100,000+ students during the back-to-college season at rec centers and food halls.

“I feel like I was back home on a summer day — this is going on my next grocery haul! — Pure Leaf sampler
Key Differentiator — Scaled Network
10,000+ trusted venues.
Zero brand ambassadors.

We don't hire temporary brand ambassadors. Products are delivered by the people your consumers already trust — the fitness instructor, the front desk staff, the housing coordinator. Trusted voices in trusted environments.

K-8 Schools
912+ schools. Parents at pick-up and drop-off, every school day.
University Housing
493+ locations. Door-to-door to students building lifelong habits.

Events & Festivals
85K+ attendees at a single activation. High-energy brand moments.
Youth Sports & Rec
250+ rec centers. Active families at their most engaged.
Retail Proximity
Activations geo-targeted near your highest-volume store clusters.
Summer & Travel
Camps, travel hubs, and rec areas during peak family season.

your brand can use.

Key Differentiator — Omnichannel Amplification
Every sample comes with a media plan built in.

Every Recess activation unlocks venue-owned social posts, direct email to their member base, on-site signage, and consumer UGC tagging your brand — a multi-touch program that extends every sample far beyond the initial handoff.

Key Differentiator — Retailer-Aligned Targeting
Every sample is geo-targeted to drive consumers to the nearest shelf.

Recess doesn't sample randomly. Every activation is geo-fenced around priority retailer locations — ensuring the consumers who try your product can buy it within miles of where they sampled it.

🏫
University Housing
→
Walmart & Target near campus
🏋
Fitness Studios
→
Kroger & Whole Foods nearby
💻
Coworking Spaces
→
Target & convenience within blocks
🏠
Apartments & Events
→
Regional grocers & mass retail
$5.10
iROAS measured on Mountain Dew Dragon Fruit — tied to Walmart innovation strategy
Key Differentiator — Closed-Loop Retail Measurement
Real measurement, not vanity metrics.

Most sampling partners report how many units they distributed. Recess reports what those units actually did — purchase intent, new-to-brand conversion, and iROAS measured through consumer insights surveys and retailer data.

📈
Purchase Intent
Measured through post-sampling consumer surveys — what percentage of samplers intend to purchase your product within 30 days.
🌟
New-to-Brand Conversion
Identify what percentage of samplers had never tried your brand before — proof that sampling is reaching new consumers, not preaching to the choir.
💰
iROAS & Incremental Sales
Every campaign measured to incremental return on ad spend — tying physical samples to actual retail purchase behavior through closed-loop data.
📊
Consumer Insights Reports
Taste feedback, packaging perception, brand awareness lift, and competitive switching data — delivered as a full insights report after every campaign.
Key Differentiator — Moments That Shape Habits
Own every key consumer moment in 2026
before your competitors do.

Every PepsiCo brand has a key consumer moment. Tell us your season and we'll map the exact venues, audiences, and activations that make it convert.

Summer
Summer & Festivals
Peak hydration and snacking season. Music festivals, outdoor fitness events, summer camps — the highest-volume sampling season where brand moments become lifelong habits.

Aug – Sep
Back to College
Students are forming new routines. University housing, campus rec centers, move-in events — the moment when beverage and snack habits get established for the entire year ahead.

Year-Round
Fitness & Wellness
Health-conscious consumers at their most receptive — post-workout, pre-class, in the studio lobby. The exact context where better-for-you positioning converts to trial and purchase.

What's Next
The playbook is already built across PepsiCo. Your brand is next.

Every campaign above used the same model — trusted venues, integrated activation, omnichannel amplification, and measured results. We don't start from scratch. We plug your brand into a system that's already proven across the PepsiCo portfolio, geo-targeted to your priority retailers and consumer moments.

One investment. Every stakeholder served.

PepsiCo brands are investing in trial at the highest level in years. Recess connects that investment to retailer-specific growth — every activation is geo-targeted around high-density retailer locations, measured through consumer insights and purchase intent data, and designed to benefit the brand, the retailer, and the shopper team simultaneously.

Your brand investment isn't just driving trial — it's driving measurable impact at Walmart, Target, Kroger, and thousands of retail partners. One investment. Multiple stakeholders served. One clear report that proves it.

1

National Brand Investment

Brand marketing funds a national sampling program to drive trial and awareness at scale

2

Recess Activates in Communities

Targeted to venues where your consumers gather — campuses, gyms, coworking spaces, events. Trusted venue staff distribute product + social & email follow-up drives to brand loyalty.

3

Measured to the Retailer Shelf

Consumer insights, purchase intent, new-to-brand conversion, and social amplification — tied to specific retail banners. One program. One report. Every stakeholder wins.

Built for every team at the table
Brand Marketing
National reach with community-level targeting. Trial, awareness, and purchase intent all measured.
Shopper Marketing
Programs that drive consumers from sample to shelf, with consumer insights to prove it.
Customer Teams
Turnkey programs for retailer conversations — activations tied directly to retailer performance goals and geo-targeted to priority store locations.
Social Teams
UGC from real campaigns without hiring influencers. Real consumers, authentic moments, organic reach.
